ai.hero_subtext: Find addiction and mental-health treatment in {state} that works with your insurance.
ai.intro_html:
If you're looking for drug or alcohol treatment in {state}, your health insurance may help cover the cost. {business} is a free service that helps you understand your benefits and find treatment options—so you can focus on getting help, not navigating paperwork.

Whether you have coverage through your employer, the individual marketplace, Medicaid, or another plan, we'll help you figure out what's available to you and connect you with resources in your area.

feature_points.heading: Understand your benefits
feature_points.text: We help you learn what your specific plan covers for addiction and mental-health treatment—no surprises later.

feature_points.heading: Find treatment options
feature_points.text: {state} offers inpatient, outpatient, and telehealth programs; we'll help you explore what's available and covered.

feature_points.heading: Get connected for free
feature_points.text: Our service is completely free—we're here to help you access the benefits you've already paid for.

faq.q: Does my {state} insurance cover addiction treatment?
faq.a: Most health plans in {state} are required to cover addiction and mental-health treatment under state and federal parity laws. The specifics—like whether inpatient or outpatient care is covered, your deductible, and copays—depend on your individual plan. We'll help you find out what your plan includes.

faq.q: How do I know what my plan will actually pay for?
faq.a: The best way is to verify your benefits directly by calling the number on your insurance card or checking your online account. We can guide you through what questions to ask. Coverage varies by plan, so it's important to confirm your own benefits before starting treatment.

faq.q: What types of treatment are available in {state}?
faq.a: {state} offers inpatient (residential) programs, intensive outpatient programs, standard outpatient therapy, telehealth counseling, and medication-assisted treatment, among other options. The right fit depends on your needs, and your insurance may cover multiple types of care.

faq.q: I'm in crisis right now. What should I do?
faq.a: If you're in immediate danger, call 911. For substance use or mental-health support, call or text 988 (Suicide and Crisis Lifeline) anytime—it's free and available 24/7. {business} can help you plan next steps once you're safe.

ai.local_note: {state} has a range of addiction and mental-health treatment options across the state, with coverage varying by insurance plan. Start by verifying your benefits, then we'll help you connect with resources that accept your insurance.

ai.hero_subtext: Understanding your coverage options for addiction and mental-health treatment in {state}.
ai.intro_html:
If you're looking for drug and alcohol rehab in {state}, your insurance may help cover the cost. Whether you have a job-based plan, Medicaid, Medicare, or another type of coverage, understanding what your specific plan includes is the first step toward getting care.

{business} is a free service that helps you understand your benefits and find treatment options that work with your insurance. We're here to answer your questions—no judgment, no sales pitch.

feature_points.heading: Verify your own benefits first
feature_points.text: Call the number on the back of your insurance card to ask what addiction and mental-health treatment your plan covers, including inpatient, outpatient, and telehealth options.

feature_points.heading: {state} has parity protections
feature_points.text: State and federal law requires insurers to cover mental-health and addiction treatment at rates similar to medical care, though specific coverage varies by plan.

feature_points.heading: Multiple treatment types available
feature_points.text: {state} offers residential programs, intensive outpatient care, standard outpatient counseling, medication-assisted treatment, and virtual options—many of which accept insurance.

faq.q: Does my {state} insurance cover addiction treatment?
faq.a: Most health plans do cover some form of addiction treatment, but what's covered depends on your specific plan and policy. Call your insurance company to find out what's included, such as inpatient rehab, outpatient therapy, or medication-assisted treatment.

faq.q: How do I know if a treatment facility accepts my insurance?
faq.a: Ask the facility directly what insurance plans they work with, and then verify with your insurance company that the facility is in-network or covered. Your insurance company can also provide a list of in-network providers.

faq.q: What should I ask my insurance company?
faq.a: Ask about deductibles, copays, out-of-pocket limits, inpatient versus outpatient coverage, whether pre-authorization is needed, and which treatment facilities or providers are in-network. Write down the date, time, and name of the representative you speak with.

faq.q: What if I'm in crisis right now?
faq.a: Call or text 988 (Suicide and Crisis Lifeline) any time, day or night. If you're in immediate danger, call 911 or go to your nearest emergency room. You can get help today without waiting to sort out insurance details.

ai.local_note: {state} has a range of treatment facilities statewide, many of which accept insurance. Start by verifying your coverage with your insurance company, then ask facilities which plans they accept.

faq.q: How do I know if my insurance covers addiction treatment in {state}?
faq.a: The best way is to call {business} and speak with someone who can review your specific plan. Insurance coverage varies widely by plan type, employer, and state regulations. We can help you find out what your benefits actually include before you contact a treatment provider.

faq.q: Do I have to pay out of pocket for treatment?
faq.a: That depends on your individual plan. Some plans cover all or most treatment costs; others have copays, coinsurance, or deductibles you'll pay. We help you understand your costs upfront so there are no surprises. Always verify your exact benefits with your insurance carrier or our team before starting treatment.

faq.q: What if I don't have insurance?
faq.a: If you're uninsured or underinsured, {state} has community health centers, sliding-scale programs, and state-funded treatment options. We can point you toward resources and help you understand what's available in your area.

Yes. {business} is a free, confidential referral service. We never judge and we don't share your information without your permission. If you're in crisis right now, call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) or call 911.

faq.q: Does my {state} health insurance cover addiction treatment?
faq.a: Most health insurance plans cover some form of addiction and mental-health treatment under federal parity law. Coverage details—what type of treatment, how long, and your out-of-pocket costs—depend on your specific plan. We help you verify your benefits so you know what's covered before you search for treatment.

faq.q: What types of treatment are available in {state}?
faq.a: {state} offers outpatient programs, intensive outpatient programs (IOP), residential treatment, and inpatient hospitalization for addiction and co-occurring mental health conditions. Your insurance, clinical needs, and personal preferences will all shape which option is right for you. We can help you explore what your plan covers.

faq.q: How do I get started if I'm in crisis?
faq.a: If you or someone else is in immediate danger, call 911. For substance use or mental-health crisis support, call or text 988 (Suicide and Crisis Lifeline) anytime, day or night. Once you're safe, {business} can help you understand your insurance and find ongoing treatment.

We're a free information and referral service. You tell us about your insurance and situation, we help you verify your benefits, and we connect you with treatment programs in {state} that accept your plan. We're not a treatment provider, insurance company, or broker—just here to make the process clearer.
